title: rosa glauca var. subcollina
intro: Rosa glauca var. subcollina, also known as the rose of Rabbat, is a lesser-known variety of the Rosa glauca species, a type of shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The rose hips of Rosa glauca var. subcollina have been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to promote digestion, reduce stress, and provide immune system support. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage.
